title: Piquant Italian Spaghetti Cacio e Pepe
description: A charred, gooey twist on classic Cacio e Pepe, packed with sesame seed oil tadka and served in a mason jar for meal prep.
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti until al dente (about 8-10 minutes).
While pasta cooks, heat the sesame seed oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat.
Add cumin seeds and chili flakes to the oil; fry until fragrant and slightly browned, about 30 seconds.
Stir in fresh ground black pepper and garlic powder, cooking for another 30 seconds to release aroma.
Remove the skillet from heat and let the tadka cool for a minute; this preserves the black pepper's charred flavor.
Drain the spaghetti and reserve 1 cup of pasta water.
Toss the hot spaghetti with the tadka, grated cheese, lemon zest, and a splash of reserved pasta water until the cheese melts and forms a creamy sauce.
Transfer the creamy pasta into a clean mason jar, sealing it tightly.
Refrigerate the jar for at least 30 minutes to allow flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken.
Before serving, stir the pasta well; if too thick, add a tablespoon of pasta water to loosen.
Garnish with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds and extra black pepper.
Serve chilled or at room temperature for a satisfying low-calorie meal.
